description: To whisper: to speak in breath instead of voice, so the words reach one listener and let the night alone. Secrets at bedtime, games after the lamp is lit, comfort at a sickbed, the voice's way of standing close. 'Whishela' is the quietest complete speech, talk that has taken off its shoes.
sound_symbolism: The word is made of the sounds whispering is made of: 'wh' /ʍ/ pure breath, 'sh' /ʃ/ the hush itself, and only then 'la', the small flow of what gets said. Spoken aloud it barely rises above its own meaning; it cannot be shouted without stopping being itself.
grammatical_notes: Verb of speech, patterning with 'haolu' (speak): it frames quotation ('shola ... sholo whishela', whispered these words) and takes a hearer with 'wei' ('shia wei lopia whishela.', they whisper to the child). The event noun, a whisper, comes by rule. Sits at the quiet end of the voice's scale, opposite 'kapura' (shout); 'maeli haolu' (speak quietly) is manner, while 'whishela' is the breath-speech itself.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Speech practiced at the threshold of silence; the whisper keeps the quiet it enters.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The gentlest register of speech given its own word: communication scaled down to exactly one trusted hearer.
